title: Redesign the main content area: Section Header, metadata, breadcrumbs
description: Per `plano-de-interface.md`'s `# أپrea principal`, `# Hierarquia tipogrأ،fica`, and `# Navegaأ§أ£o` sections, and the `referencia.png` screenshot: the editing surface should read as a document, not a form. This task rebuilds `PageContentPage.tsx`'s header into a proper 'Section Header' component (category eyebrow, large title, optional description, metadata row: created/updated/author, status), adds breadcrumbs above it, centers the content column with a max-width, and generally increases whitespace. This is the task that makes the editor 'feel like editing documentation, not a database' per the plan's core principle.
acceptanceCriteria: A reusable `SectionHeader` component exists with: category/eyebrow label (the page's section title, uppercase, small, muted), page or tab title (large, bold, using TASK-82's type scale), optional description, and a metadata row showing created/updated/author â€” reusing whatever `pages`/`tabs`/`revisions` data already exposes timestamps and author (check `revisions` table per CLAUDE.md's note on `autor_id`); if per-page author/timestamp data isn't currently returned by the relevant query, surface only what's available rather than inventing new backend fields

Breadcrumbs render above the Section Header: Menu > Section > Page > (Tab, if not the primary body) â€” extends the plan's `# Navegaأ§أ£o` example ('Get started > Installation > Accessibility') with the Menu level introduced by TASK-83/84/85; the Menu segment navigates back to that menu (selecting it as active, per TASK-85's mechanism)
The content column is centered with a max-width around 1200px, consistent with the plan's `# أپrea principal` spec, replacing the current full-width flex layout in `AdminLayout`'s `<main>`

Status (e.g. a Draft/Published-style tag) is visible near the title per the plan's editor example â€” reuse or derive from existing publish-state data (`pages.publish`/revisions) without inventing a new status field if one doesn't exist; if there's no draft/published boolean readily available, a minimal derived indicator (e.g. 'never published' vs 'published') is acceptable

Publicar/Histأ³rico actions remain present and contextual to the page (not moved into the header per TASK-85's 'no edit actions in header' rule) â€” this task only restyles their container, it doesn't change what they do
Visual regression check: dos-donts, callout, code, table, and component-embed blocks (Fase 3/8) still render correctly inside the wider/centered layout

steps.description: Check what metadata is actually available
steps.details: Inspect `pages`/`tabs`/`revisions` schema and the tRPC procedures already used by `PageContentPage` (`tabs.getPrimary`, `tabs.listByPage`) plus revision history endpoints (TASK-34/36/69) for created/updated/author fields. Extend a query only if trivial (e.g. exposing an existing column not yet selected) â€” do not design new tracking.

New shared component(s) under `apps/admin/src/components/` (or `features/editor/`) consuming TASK-82's typography primitives. Breadcrumbs derive from the section/page/tab already loaded by `PageContentPage` (and available to `SidebarTree`'s query cache).

Swap the current `<h1>` + inline Publicar/Histأ³rico row for the new `SectionHeader`; adjust `AdminLayout`'s `<main>` to center content at the ~1200px max-width per the plan, increasing whitespace/padding.

Open a page containing each block type (heading/paragraph/list/code/image/table/callout/component-embed/dos-donts) and confirm layout/wrapping still looks correct at the new max-width; re-check responsiveness isn't broken (full responsive pass is TASK-92).

technicalNotes: This is the highest-leverage task for the plan's core principle ('conteأ؛do أ© o protagonista') â€” prioritize whitespace and restraint over adding new UI elements.

Don't invent backend fields for metadata that doesn't exist; better to omit a field than fake data. Cross-check against the `revisions` table nullable `autor_id` note in CLAUDE.md before assuming author is always available.
Keep Publicar/Histأ³rico functionally identical (same mutations/routes) â€” this task is presentation only for that part.
